prevalence.trend: increasing due to rising obesity and aging population

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Lyon Consensus 2018 criteria for conclusive GERD (e.g., abnormal acid exposure time on pH-metry or LA grade C/D esophagitis on endoscopy), integrated with Rome IV for overlapping functional dyspepsia

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no routine screening, Barrett's surveillance in high-risk
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 65% with adequate symptom control on PPI therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Lyon Consensus: pH-metry, endoscopy for LA grade C/D esophagitis, widespread adoption across EU gastroenterology societies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: PPIs first-line (e.g., omeprazole 20-40mg daily), H2 blockers for mild cases, fundoplication for refractory cases, Barrett's surveillance per BSG/ESGE guidelines
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: PPI overuse leading to resistance and side effects, rising obesity epidemic, surveillance burden for Barrett's esophagus progression to esophageal adenocarcinoma
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Lyon Consensus guidelines harmonized EU-wide, high endoscopy access in public health systems, comprehensive Barrett's monitoring programs in countries like UK and Germany
